name: git_push
description: Pushes committed changes from a local branch to a remote repository. Requires the remote repository and branch to be configured or specified. Ensure all desired changes are committed locally before pushing.

project_path.description: The root directory path of the project containing the Git repository.

remote_name.description: The name of the remote repository to push to (e.g., 'origin'). Defaults to 'origin'.

branch_name.description: The name of the local branch to push. If not specified, attempts to push the current branch or uses Git's default push behavior for the specified remote.

force_push.description: If true, performs a force push (git push --force). DANGEROUS: This can overwrite remote history. Use with extreme caution and only if absolutely necessary and understood.

set_upstream.description: If true, sets the upstream branch for the local branch being pushed (e.g., 'git push -u origin main'). Useful for the first push of a new branch.

hints.description: This tool is destructive as it modifies the remote repository. Force pushing is especially destructive. Not idempotent.
